purpose: Keep CRBSM, Route A, scattering-native regimes, and high-resonance/mesoscopic resonance-native regimes separated while comparing them against Formation Medium gates. This is a mechanism/comparator/route discipline plan, not a source claim or substrate selection.
anti_flattening_rule: CRBSM is mechanism-language, not just another route. Route A is hardware/proxy. Scattering-native is a mandatory comparator. High-resonance may be the bridge between CRBSM-like mechanism language and buildable/harnessable hardware. These layers must not be flattened into one route, one substrate, or one winner.

layered_candidates.name: Chiral Relay Boundary-Sheet Medium
layered_candidates.positive_function: names the candidate mechanism language: boundary sheets, chiral relay pockets, quarter-turn/A2-like local transport, persistent support/channel identity, and bulk/sheet separation.
layered_candidates.hard_question: Can persistent support/channel identity and relay-usage memory be physically native rather than constructed-by-assumption?

layered_candidates.name: topoelectrical + superconducting microwave stack
layered_candidates.positive_function: provides a source-reviewed instrumentable bootstrap stack: topoelectrical boundary/corner impedance readout plus tunable/coherent superconducting microwave transport/coupling.
layered_candidates.hard_question: Can hardware/proxy components support CRBSM-like mechanism primitives without collapsing into external routing, measurement, or ordinary simulation?
blocked_flattenings: Route A as Formation Medium implemented
Route A source review as engineering feasibility

layered_candidates.name: scattering-native / S-matrix regimes
layered_candidates.positive_function: keeps the closest amplitude/positive-geometry ontology in comparison; tests whether the native laws already carry the relevant readout more directly than engineered proxy mechanisms.
layered_candidates.hard_question: Can scattering-native structure be harnessed with carrier, transport, persistence, apparatus coupling, and intrinsic readout, rather than remaining only a mathematical/native-law comparator?
blocked_flattenings: high-energy scattering as selected substrate by mathematical proximity
S-matrix ontology as Formation Medium proof

layered_candidates.name: high-resonance / mesoscopic resonance-native regimes
layered_candidates.layer_label: possible bridge comparator between mechanism-language and buildable hardware
layered_candidates.positive_function: keeps open the possibility that resonance-native mesoscopic regimes supply carrier, relay/transport, persistence, readout, and harnessability more naturally than high-energy scattering or ordinary hardware routing.
layered_candidates.hard_question: Can high-resonance regimes provide native carrier, native relay/transport, persistent support identity, intrinsic readout, and bulk/readout separation without being mere controlled resonance hardware?
blocked_flattenings: Route A resonance hardware as high-resonance-native Formation Medium
tunable resonance as native settling law

preliminary_matrix.failure_mode: constructed-by-assumption mechanism language treated as physics
external routing/readout stack mistaken for native Formation Medium
mathematical proximity treated as substrate selection
tunable resonance/control hardware mistaken for native mechanism

required_next_reviews.question: Which mesoscopic high-resonance systems have long-lived or metastable channel/mode identity that is native rather than software-tracked?
required_next_reviews.required_output: source-intake plan or source review, not source-extracted claim until sources are ingested

required_next_reviews.question: Can boundary/corner/resonant modes become boundary sheets or relay pockets rather than mere measured modes?
required_next_reviews.required_output: negative-control comparison against Route A source review

required_next_reviews.question: Can high-resonance transport support chiral/quarter-turn-like relay behavior without programmed routing?
Does scattering-native physics still dominate when scored against harnessability/buildability, persistence, and intrinsic readout?
required_next_reviews.required_output: comparator matrix update, not winner declaration

kill_conditions: CRBSM is treated as selected substrate
Route A is treated as CRBSM implementation
scattering-native is treated as default winner
high-resonance is treated as default winner
tunable resonance is treated as native settling law
hardware readout is treated as intrinsic canonical readout
source review is treated as source extraction
non_promotion_rule: This plan is role discipline and comparator planning only. It does not prove Formation Medium, validate CRBSM, select Route A, select scattering-native physics, select high-resonance regimes, prove a substrate, prove a settling law, or authorize corporate/investor claims.
